newsSince: Material positive. On June 22-23, MediaTek reportedly won the exclusive Google TPU v9 'Triggerfish' contract via its 336G proprietary SerDes (Google's 448G hit signal-integrity/power/thermal issues; Broadcom temporarily lost ground), at ~+30% unit pricing and 1-2M extra units on top of 4-5M Humufish; it also signaled broad 10-20% price hikes. 2026 AI-ASIC revenue forecast lifted to ~$2B, targeting 10-15% ASIC share by 2027.

sources.label: MediaTek wins TPU v9 on SerDes shift, +10-20% pricing (TrendForce)
MediaTek ASIC revenue may overtake smartphone chips on Google TPU (DigiTimes)

verdicts.rankRationale: New evidence challenges the prior 'analog/edge, low-directness' framing: June 22-23 TPU v9 Triggerfish exclusive win (336G SerDes, +30% price, +1-2M units) plus AI-ASIC revenue raised to ~$2B materially upgrades MediaTek's AI-accelerator/datacenter directness on this node-criticality list. Up from the de-rank.
verdicts.bull: Exclusive TPU v9 Triggerfish win on 336G SerDes; AI-ASIC rev ~$2B 2026, targeting 10-15% ASIC share; Broadcom lost ground.
verdicts.neutral: Still smartphone-led; ASIC is fast-growing but a minority of revenue at +208% YTD.
verdicts.bear: Single-customer (Google) ASIC concentration; consumer cycle; extreme YTD re-rating.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: TPU v9 Triggerfish/Humufish volume ramp + ASIC customer diversification.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Google design-revision/sole-customer risk; consumer SoC cycle.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the CPO-specific lens MediaTek is still a self-admitted CPO 'latecomer' (Ayar Labs stake a placeholder, demand ~2028-29). The TPU v9 ASIC win is interconnect/ASIC alpha, not co-packaged-optics, so only a slight positive here; it remains AI-edge/ASIC optionality rather than a primary CPO bottleneck.
verdicts.bull: Datacenter SerDes/ASIC momentum gives optionality adjacent to optical interconnect.
verdicts.neutral: ASIC win is real but not CPO-specific; CPO remains 2028-29.
verdicts.bear: Self-described CPO latecomer; no primary CPO bottleneck position, +208% YTD.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: High-speed SerDes leadership feeding into optical-interconnect roadmap.
verdicts.primaryRisk: CPO demand not material until 2028-29; not a primary CPO play.
